Ad Text:
We are seeking an experienced Power BI Lead to drive data visualisation and reporting initiatives for a large mining client. This temporary role based in Brisbane offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact on critical technology projects.

Client Details

This role is within a large organisation operating in the Mining industry. With a strong focus on leveraging cutting-edge technology, the company is committed to delivering innovative solutions to support its operations and strategic goals.

Description

Role and Responsibilities:

· Provide PowerBI, Analysis for Office (AfO) visualisation subject matter expertise in translating business requirements into quality, sustainable solutions

· Convert business requirements into workable functional/ technical design and realistic estimates

· Design, build, configure, implement and test quality technical solutions according to Rio Tinto standards

· Provide guidance and governance for identified remediation work

· Work with other team members in a collaborative manner, freely sharing expertise with others

· Responsible for competing tasks on time and to cost and quality specifications

· Identify Project risks and issues, addressed & resolved in a timely manner

· Ensure outcomes meet customer expectations and achieve sign-off for the successful completion of all agreed deliverables

Profile

Key Criteria:

· Demonstrated 8 +years of solid PowerBI visualisation and dataset expertise in large, complex enterprise environments

· Deep understanding of design principles for sustainable solution architecture.

· Experience in divestment and restructuring strategies and impacts on SAP and BW/4HANA systems and downstream reporting solutions

· Experience of integrating with BW/4HANA data sources (preferred) and the creation and update of SAP Analysis for Office workbooks

· Ability to guide contractors and vendors on technical deliverables including governance

· Customer-focused and collaborative across cultures and virtual teams.

· Highly developed written and oral communication skills

· Ability to use influence and build relationships with internal and external teams

· Ability to work in complex organisational environments with remote teams

Job Offer

Competitive daily rate.
Opportunity to work within a leading organisation in the mining industry.
Be part of a collaborative and innovative technology department.
Convenient Brisbane location.
Chance to contribute to impactful projects in a globally recognised organisation.

If you're ready to take the next step in your career as a Power BI Lead, we encourage you to apply today.

To apply online please click the 'Apply' button below. For a confidential discussion about this role please contact Harley Logan at harleylogan@michaelpage.com.au

Senior Creative Strategist
Hommey

Where creative instinct meets performance thinking. If that is the kind of work you are drawn to, read on.

Hommey is a premium homewares brand that takes its products and its creative seriously. We are looking for a Senior Creative Strategist to take ownership of our paid creative function and help shape how we show up across paid social.

This is a role with genuine scope. You will work across strategy, production and team development, with the support of a Head of Performance and a creative team behind you.

About Us

Born in 2021 with a simple cushion and a big dream, Hommey is building a homeware brand that people genuinely love. We believe you can create something aspirational and top quality without the premium price tag, and we're proving it. Our mission is to help people craft a home that truly reflects who they are. We're a small, ambitious team and we're just getting started.

The Role

Sitting at the intersection of brand and performance, you will lead creative strategy across paid channels and work closely with a team of specialists. Reporting to the Head of Performance, your focus will be on building creative that is strategic, consistently executed and informed by data.

Day to day, you will be:

Developing and owning channel-specific creative strategy across all paid social platforms
Building and maintaining a structured testing framework so every new creative direction generates learnable, scalable outcomes
Writing scripts, concepts and copy hands-on, keeping Hommey's sensory, lifestyle-led voice intact while driving direct response
Managing end-to-end creative production from concept to delivery across paid channels
Analysing performance data including thumb-stop rate, hook rate, CTR, CPA and ROAS, and translating insights into the next brief
Sharing performance insights proactively with brand marketing, closing the loop between paid and organic creative
Leading, coaching and developing the Creative Performance Specialist and Performance Design Team
Managing UGC creator and freelancer relationships with clear briefs and quality control
Monitoring competitor creative and platform trends, feeding findings into Hommey's own strategy
 Integrating AI tools into reporting, insight generation and production workflows to extend team capacity
Who We're Looking For

We are looking for someone who is as comfortable in the data as they are in a creative brief, and who leads a team in a way that brings out the best in the people around them.

You will likely be a good fit if you:

Have 5 or more years of experience in paid social creative strategy, with proven performance results in a DTC, ecommerce or digital agency environment
Know how to develop creative that performs across paid social platforms, with a genuine grasp of what makes each channel tick
Can move between the strategic and the hands-on, writing a script and reviewing a dashboard in the same afternoon
Have experience leading or mentoring a creative team, and know how to give feedback that raises the standard of the work
Are comfortable building and scaling high-performing creative teams, from defining team structure to retaining strong talent
Bring strong analytical capability and can translate paid media data into clear, actionable creative decisions
Have proven experience with UGC strategy and creator management at scale
Write briefs that are sharp, specific and give creatives everything they need to execute without ambiguity
Have a portfolio that shows both strategic thinking and hands-on execution
It is a bonus if you have:

A background in lifestyle, home goods, fashion or beauty
Familiarity with platform-specific creative requirements across a range of channels
Working knowledge of AI tools applied to creative workflows
Experience in a high-growth start-up or scale-up environment
Why Join Hommey?

At Hommey, we're building more than a home and lifestyle brand — we're creating a movement defined by comfort, quality, and personalisation
We believe in what we do. Be part of an ambitious start-up team disrupting the homewares market
A flexible work culture is at the heart of what we do. We believe in flexibility and surrounding ourselves with passionate individuals who value creativity and teamwork
We offer Comfort Days: these are five days in addition to A/L accruals
We love our community and are customer centric. We want to create products that make a difference in the lives of our customers
Sound like the right fit?
  
We would love to hear from you. Send through your CV and a brief note on what draws you to this role.

Techtronic Industries (TTI) is a global organisation, a world-class leader in quality consumer and professional products marketed to the home improvement and construction industries. The TTI brands like MILWAUKEE, RYOBI, AEG, VAX and many others are recognised worldwide for their deep heritage, superior quality, outstanding performance and compelling innovation.
Through an ongoing company-wide commitment to cordless technology, innovation and strong customer partnerships, TTI consistently delivers exciting new products that enhance customer satisfaction and productivity. We are committed to ensuring our people have a great experience at TTI, so in addition to an exciting and challenging role we also provide:

Lifestyle: Hybrid working model to support flexibility.
Personal Development: Access to LinkedIn Learning and professional development programs.
Health & Wellbeing: Paid Parental Leave and Employee Assistance Program.
Discounts & Perks: Generous discounts on company products, laptop provided, novated leasing options.
Social: Recognition programs and social events to celebrate our team’s achievements

About the Role
Reporting to the IT Finance Systems Manager, the Anaplan Manager will own the design, development, and ongoing management of TTI’s Anaplan models. This role partners closely with Finance, Operations, Sales, and IT to deliver scalable, high‑performing planning and modelling solutions that support strategic decision‑making and business growth.

You will play a key role in translating complex business requirements into robust Anaplan solutions, ensuring data integrity, performance optimisation, and strong user adoption across the organisation.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end‑to‑end Anaplan model design, build, and optimisation in line with best practices.
Partner with stakeholders to understand planning, forecasting, and reporting requirements.
Integrate and transform data from multiple source systems, ensuring accuracy and consistency.
Maintain and enhance existing models to reflect evolving business needs.
Conduct testing, validation, and quality assurance of all models.
Provide user training, support, and comprehensive model documentation.
Liaise with Anaplan Support and software vendors to resolve issues within SLA.
Contribute financial system expertise to broader business and technology initiatives.
Participate in and lead small to medium projects alongside BAU activities.

About You

You are a confident Anaplan leader who enjoys working at the intersection of finance, systems, and technology. You bring a strong attention to detail, a collaborative mindset, and the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels.

Key requirements include:

7+ years’ experience as an Anaplan Modeler with deep platform expertise.
Proven experience designing complex Anaplan models, dashboards, and workflows.
Anaplan certification(s) – mandatory.
Strong understanding of financial and commercial concepts.
Solid business analysis and process improvement experience.
Excellent problem‑solving and communication skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities across projects and BAU.

If you are excited at the prospect of joining a large, successful and dynamic organization, then please apply now!

Job Title: Associate Program Director, Data & AI Transformation
Ad Text:
Associate Program Director, Data & AI Transformation

Permanent Full Time | 5 weeks annual leave + monthly ADO
Based at Box Hill | Local parks, shops and cafes
Lead a team of data scientists and engineers delivering data solutions and advanced analytics

Eastern Health

Eastern Health is one of Melbourne's largest public health services. We provide a range of emergency, surgical, medical and general healthcare services and have a proud history of serving a diverse community.

Our Employee Value Proposition is "Being a part of Eastern Health means belonging to a welcoming team of healthcare experts".

This statement aligns with our promise to our communities, patients, consumers and staff that we will be HEALTHIER TOGETHER. Bolder than a vision for the future, our promise calls us to action. We know that working together is the only way we can achieve what is necessary for a healthier future.

About the role

The Associate Program Director, Data Engineering & Science provides strategic leadership and operational oversight for a team responsible for delivering governed, high-quality data solutions and advanced analytics. This role focuses on management, governance, and coordination, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned to organisational priorities.

About you

To be successful in this role you will possess:

Excellent technical skillset, minimally with proven expertise in Python and SQL
Proven ability to build predictive models that are adopted by the organisation
Up to date knowledge of statistical and machine learning techniques
Expertise in leveraging both structured and unstructured data sources
Outstanding problem-solving skills including the ability to work with ambiguity
Proven ability to communicate complex results to technical and non-technical audiences utilising data visualisation and storytelling skills
Excellent knowledge of how data can be used to support decision making
Appropriate tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science or a related quantitative field
Demonstrated experience managing multi-disciplinary data teams (engineering and analytics)
Proven ability to implement and maintain data governance frameworks
Strong understanding of project governance methodologies
Experience with public health datasets, including privacy and compliance requirements
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Healthcare workers are strongly recommended to follow COVID vaccination recommendations provided in the Australian Immunisation handbook (based on ATAGI advice). Seasonal vaccination against influenza is a mandatory requirement of this role and an offer is conditional on this being up to date prior to employment.

What we offer

Eastern Health offers a variety of fantastic Employee Benefits, view the extensive range here!

Mentor and Shadowing program
Access to discovery days across our 50 sites
Study and professional development leave
Friendly & supportive teams
Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
Fitness Passport for work-life balance
Employee Assistance program accessible for you and your loved ones
Flexible working hours and excellent entitlements
Salary packaging options and an employee benefits scheme
Access to Private Health Insurance offers
Digital upskilling, such as excel and Power-Bi training
Cafes at all of our Hospitals

Next Steps

Apply online if you feel we are a good match, you have the skills and qualities to make a difference, and are eligible to work in Australia.

We review all applications when we receive them, and we notify unsuccessful candidates.

If you are shortlisted, we will be in touch to arrange an interview, and you must complete a National Police Check.

Good to know:

We are an equal opportunity employer and actively promote equality for people of all races, ethnicities, gender identities, sexual orientations, and for people living with disability - within the communities we serve and our Eastern Health team.
We provide a culturally safe and welcoming environment for Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander employees.
As a child-safe organisation, we ensure child safety and wellbeing is embedded in our everyday culture.
Vaccination and screening for immunity to vaccine-preventable disease is a mandatory requirement of this role.
Advertising may end before the closing date.
We do not accept recruitment agency application

If you are a current Eastern Health employee please ensure you log in with your internal Eastern Health account to apply for this or other roles.

Contact Person: Suma Shivalingaiah - CIO

Contact Email: Suma.Shivalingaiah@easternhealth.org.au

Respect for all | Safe always | Partnering in care | Learning and improving every day

Company: Sharp & Carter Technology, Projects & Digital Brisbane
Ad Text:
We are seeking an experienced Delivery Lead to drive delivery across a growing digital portfolio including websites, portals, digital forms, and digital experience platforms.
 
This role sits within a large-scale transformation environment with multiple initiatives running concurrently. The Delivery Lead will work across a dynamic pipeline of digital work, supporting both in-flight delivery and shaping upcoming initiatives.
 
You will operate in a hands-on capacity, working closely with delivery teams, business stakeholders, and technical resources to drive outcomes across a fast-moving digital domain.
 
The Role:
This role will initially focus on supporting the delivery and enhancement of website uplift and digital experience platform, while also shaping upcoming work across forms, portals, and broader digital capabilities.
The environment is fast-paced with competing priorities, requiring someone comfortable balancing planning with reactive delivery leadership.
 
Key responsibilities include:

Leading delivery across digital initiatives (website, portals, forms, DXP)
Supporting completion and enhancement of a new digital experience platform
Managing competing priorities across multiple concurrent initiatives
Working closely with delivery teams including analysts, testers and SMEs
Supporting planning, cost estimates and business case inputs
Coordinating work across busy internal stakeholder groups
Driving delivery outcomes in a fast-moving environment
Managing risks, issues and dependencies
Supporting roadmap planning for upcoming digital initiatives
Collaborating with program and project leadership
About You:
We're looking for a pragmatic Delivery Lead with strong digital delivery experience and the ability to operate across planning and execution.
 
You will bring:

Experience delivering digital initiatives (web, portals, forms, DXP)
Delivery Lead, Project Manager, or hybrid background
Experience in fast-paced, multi-project environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills
Ability to manage competing priorities across teams
Hands-on delivery leadership style
Experience working with analysts, testers and delivery teams
Agile experience (not necessarily a purist environment)
Comfortable working with evolving tooling (Jira or similar)
Ability to support business case and planning activities
Environment:

Large-scale transformation underway
Multiple digital initiatives in flight
High stakeholder engagement
Fast-moving delivery pipeline
Collaborative delivery leadership model
Blend of planning and hands-on delivery
For more information or a copy of the briefing notes, please reach out to Max at mpower@sharpandcarter.com.au

Category:
Strategy & Planning (Consulting & Strategy)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About SA Water

SA Water is a successful, modern water utility wholly owned by the Government of South Australia. We employ more than 1,700 people across a broad range of disciplines who operate more than $14 billion worth of infrastructure to deliver essential water and sewerage services to more than 1.8 million South Australians.

With operations and offices across the state, our strength and expertise have been shaped by our history. For more than 165 years, South Australians have depended on us to innovate and deliver efficient and sustainable water services. With nationally and internationally recognised industry expertise, we’re focused on understanding and meeting our customers’ expectations.

As well as safely delivering smart and reliable services for our customers of today, we innovate to shape the water solutions of the future that will support thriving economies and healthy communities.

We are driven by our values; we value our people, we do work that matters, we keep it real, we know that our work has impact, and we are future focussed. Staying true to our values ensures that we consistently deliver positive outcomes for all South Australians.

About the job:

We are seeking an experienced Infrastructure Planning & Strategy professional to partner with the Senior Manager, Infrastructure Planning & Strategy (IP&S) in delivering high-quality strategic, functional, and technical support. This role plays a key part in driving the delivery of the IP&S business plan and advancing the organisation’s long-term infrastructure strategy.

The role operates across complex planning, analytical, and stakeholder engagement environments, contributing sound advice, insight, and practical solutions to enable robust decision-making and successful outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Provide strategic, functional and technical support to the Senior Manager, Infrastructure Planning & Strategy (IP&S) to enable delivery of the IP&S business plan.
Support executive, Board and ministerial requirements through the preparation of high-quality papers, briefings, reports, communications and presentations.
Contribute to the development, monitoring and reporting of IP&S performance metrics, priorities and governance processes.
Coordinate and support the delivery of strategic initiatives, ensuring effective execution, reporting and alignment with organisational objectives.
Drive continuous improvement by identifying and delivering process and business enhancements that improve efficiency, quality and compliance.
Monitor the external environment and provide strategic insight on emerging risks, opportunities and enablers impacting infrastructure planning and strategy.

About the Person

You are a strategically focused, commercially minded professional with strong technical expertise and the ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders. You adopt a proactive, solutions-oriented approach and are comfortable supporting executives on high-priority and sensitive issues, translating complex information into clear and impactful messages.

Additional requirements are:

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, science or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience.
Demonstrated experience in strategic asset management, risk management, infrastructure investment and financial management.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence and produce high-quality executive-level documentation.
Proven ability to collaborate effectively across large, complex organisations to deliver outcomes in the best interests of the business.
Proactive, self-directed and accountable, with a strong sense of ownership and initiative.
Ability to navigate ambiguity and complexity, driving clarity, structure and informed decision-making.
Experience or knowledge of utility infrastructure, systems thinking, and the use of data analytics and visualisation tools (including AI) to support strategic insight.

Our Industry is like no other. Dive into the wonderful world of water and explore the boundless opportunities. Learn more about the water industry at careersinwater.com.au

Further information can be found in the attached PD -  Principal Strategy Adviser-PD.docx

Application Information: To apply, click the 'Apply' button and follow the prompts.

Please submit and complete the application form and upload a current resume by 5pm, 8th of May. Applications received after the closing date may not be considered. If you are experiencing difficulties submitting your application or have enquiries, please contact Gregg Forte on Gregg.forte@sawater.com.au from the Talent Team for support prior to the closing date.

A great customer experience is best achieved by having a diverse and inclusive team that reflects the community we serve. Applications are encouraged from everyone, including people living with disability, job seekers of all ages, members of the LGBTQ+ community, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and people from culturally diverse backgrounds.

Please note: We request that recruitment agencies do not submit candidates for this position, as they will not be accepted.

Company Description

Fairness feels good

Make a real impact at AFCA. Where fairness drives every decision. Help us deliver world-class, independent complaints resolution for Australians. As a not-for-profit and progressive financial ombudsman, we’re championing positive change. Achieving our purpose takes progressive thinking, accountability and resilience. At AFCA, our inclusive leadership values every voice. We offer our people flexible work options, thoughtful benefits and opportunities to deepen expertise. Flourish in a diverse, caring culture. Feel the difference of belonging to an organisation intentionally designed to put people first.

Job Description

At AFCA, our work supports fairness for Australians and strong systems matter. As Senior Delivery Lead, you’ll play a critical role in delivering a large‑scale ERP transformation that underpins how our people and services operate every day.

This role is accountable for the end‑to‑end delivery of new Human Resources, Payroll and Finance platforms, bridging complex business process transformation with technology delivery. Operating in a regulated environment, you’ll drive modern delivery practices while maintaining strong governance, stakeholder confidence and lasting adoption. This is a 2 year max term contract at AFCA.

What you’ll do

Own and lead the full delivery of the ERP transformation across HRS, Payroll and Finance platforms
Translate strategic intent into clear delivery plans, milestones and measurable outcomes
Establish and lead scaled agile (SAFe) delivery structures, integrated with enterprise governance requirements
Act as the central integrator across business process redesign, change, data and technology delivery
Partner closely with HR, Finance, Payroll, Technology and Operations leaders to ensure solutions meet business and regulatory needs
Lead and coordinate cross‑functional delivery teams, including internal staff, vendors and system integrators
Manage delivery risks, dependencies, budgets and benefits realisation across a complex transformation program
Provide clear, proactive communication and assurance to executives, steering committees and governance forums
Act as a hands‑on change leader, supporting organisational readiness, adoption and capability uplift
Embed a culture of continuous improvement, accountability and modern ways of working
Qualifications

What you'll bring:

Proven experience delivering complex, technology‑enabled transformation
Leadership of large‑scale ERP or core enterprise system implementations (HR, Payroll and Finance experience highly regarded)
Strong transformation background, spanning business process change and technology delivery end‑to‑end
Deep experience delivering at scale using Agile and SAFe in enterprise environments
Comfortable operating in regulated or highly complex organisations
Experienced leading blended delivery models across internal teams and vendors/system integrators
Hands‑on leader with strong execution discipline and outcome focus
Confident and influential stakeholder manager, including at executive level
Brings strong risk, dependency and issue management capability
Clear communicator who simplifies complexity and builds trust
A resilient, pragmatic change leader who brings people through transformation, not just systems live
Additional Information

What's on offer

BOSS Best Places to Work 2024 –credited for its culture, engagement and flexible working arrangements. 
Most Inclusive Workplace 2024 – Australian HR Institute (AHRI) Awards. 
Employer of Choice Public Sector and NFP – Australian HR Awards 2023 
Bronze AWEI Accreditation 2024 – Recognised for LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion. 
Accredited Family Friendly Workplace – Supporting work-life balance and inclusivity. 
Hybrid working – Flexible arrangements with state-of-the-art offices designed for collaboration and wellbeing. 
Inclusive leave options – Flexible public holidays, 20 weeks paid parental leave, gender affirmation leave, women’s health leave, and paid time off over Christmas. 
Financial benefits – Not-for-profit salary packaging to boost take-home pay. 
Locations – A team of over 1,500 dedicated professionals based in modern Melbourne and Sydney CBD offices. 

To apply

If you’re passionate about fairness and believe your skills align with this role, we encourage you to apply—even if you don’t meet every single criterion. 

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and gender identities. If you require any accessibility support during the recruitment process, please reach out to our team at Careers@afca.org.au. 

We believe fairness starts with people. That’s why we don’t use AI or automated tools to screen candidates. As a result, our processes may take a little longer, and we thank you for your patience. 

AFCA is a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we are committed to responding to every applicant.

Want to bring ideas to life?

At Aurecon, we turn technical curiosity into real‑world outcomes. Our aviation teams work alongside airport clients to navigate complexity, test ideas and deliver trusted planning advice that supports efficient operations and long‑term infrastructure decisions. We bring together deep technical expertise, strong collaboration and practical insight to help shape airports today and into the future.




How will your ideas contribute to shaping what's next?

We're looking for an Airport Planner to join our aviation planning and simulation capability. This role is suited to an experienced airport planner with strong analytical capability who enjoys working directly with clients and applying modelling and simulation to inform planning outcomes.

You'll play a key role in delivering airport planning and terminal simulation work across Australia and New Zealand, supporting existing client relationships and contributing to the continued development of Aurecon's airport planning and simulation offering. What you'll do

Deliver airport planning and advisory projects across areas including terminal planning, demand/capacity and level‑of‑service assessments
Apply airport simulation and analytical methodologies to support real‑world planning and operational decisions
Use simulation or modelling tools (such as CAST or comparable platforms) to test scenarios and assess performance outcomes
Translate complex technical analysis into clear, defensible recommendations for airport clients
Lead defined workstreams or components of larger multidisciplinary airport planning projects
Engage directly with clients through meetings, workshops and presentations
Support proposal development and contribute to maintaining strong, long‑term client relationships
Collaborate with planning, engineering and advisory specialists across Aurecon to deliver integrated outcomes
Support and mentor junior planners and analysts through project work and technical guidance
Contribute positively to a collaborative, supportive team culture

What we're looking for:

Demonstrated experience in airport planning, terminal planning or aviation advisory, gained within a consultancy, airport management or specialist aviation environment
Practical experience delivering studies involving passenger flows, demand and capacity analysis, level of service or operational scenario testing
Experience using simulation or analytical tools relevant to airport, transport or pedestrian planning
Strong analytical capability, with confidence working with data, assumptions and ambiguity
Proven ability to work directly with airport clients, communicating technical concepts clearly and credibly
Experience contributing to or leading workstreams within multi‑disciplinary planning projects
Background in aviation planning, civil engineering, architecture, transport planning or a related discipline
Consulting experience is strongly preferred

How will your future be shaped by us?

At Aurecon, you'll work on long‑term aviation projects with real operational impact, supporting airport clients across Australia and New Zealand. Our aviation and simulation specialists are known for building deep, trusted client relationships and delivering advice that shapes investment and planning decisions over time. You'll benefit from:

The opportunity to work on high‑impact airport projects that directly influence operational, planning and investment decisions
Meaningful involvement in shaping and strengthening Aurecon's airport simulation capability, including approaches, tools and ways of working
Regular engagement with airport clients and senior stakeholders who value strong technical insight and clear, practical advice
A collaborative, inclusive and low‑ego team culture where ideas are genuinely welcomed and acted on
Highly flexible working arrangements, including the option to work part‑time (e.g. 4 days per week) or tailor working patterns to support balance and sustainability
A long‑term career environment focused on trust, technical excellence and continued professional growth, rather than rigid structures or hierarchy



Who is Aurecon?

At Aurecon, our strength has always been our people - those who collaborate across disciplines to deliver integrated, sustainable and practical outcomes. You'll be part of a business recognised for its leadership in innovation, sustainability and inclusive culture, working alongside aviation specialists who value technical excellence and thoughtful design.

When you join Aurecon, you're joining a team which helps shape the experience, performance and longevity of the aviation ecosystem.




Ready to bring ideas to life?

Then please apply. Remember, it's you and how you bring ideas to life which are important. Your background, unique needs and individuality will be valued. Visit our website to learn more at www.aurecongroup.com. Apply now.

Company Description

Fairness feels good

Make a real impact at AFCA. Where fairness drives every decision. Help us deliver world-class, independent complaints resolution for Australians. As a not-for-profit and progressive financial ombudsman, we’re championing positive change. Achieving our purpose takes progressive thinking, accountability and resilience. At AFCA, our inclusive leadership values every voice. We offer our people flexible work options, thoughtful benefits and opportunities to deepen expertise. Flourish in a diverse, caring culture. Feel the difference of belonging to an organisation intentionally designed to put people first.

Job Description

At AFCA, delivering fairness for Australians depends on great digital experiences. As Senior Delivery Lead, you’ll play a pivotal role in transforming how our customers and stakeholders experience our services.

This role is accountable for the end‑to‑end delivery of our Digital Experience Platform (DXP) including website re‑platforming, digital applications and customer journey uplift. You’ll bring together strategy, delivery and change, ensuring initiatives deliver measurable customer, business and regulatory outcomes in a complex, fast‑paced and highly regulated environment. This is a 2 year max-term contract. 

What you’ll do

Lead the full delivery of the DXP transformation, including website re‑platforming and digital applications
Translate strategy and customer experience objectives into clear delivery plans, roadmaps and outcomes
Establish and lead scaled agile (SAFe) delivery structures aligned to enterprise governance and risk requirements
Own delivery across digital channels, content, integration, data and platform enablement
Act as the key integrator between business leaders, experience design, technology teams and delivery partners
Manage delivery risk, dependencies, budgets and benefits across a complex, multi‑stream program
Lead cross‑functional internal teams and external vendors to deliver outcomes with pace and quality
Provide clear, confident reporting and assurance to executives and governance forums
Act as a change leader, supporting adoption, capability uplift and modern ways of working
Embed a culture of continuous improvement and customer‑centred delivery
Qualifications

What you'll bring: 

Senior delivery leadership with significant experience leading complex, technology‑enabled transformation
Proven track record delivering large‑scale digital platform and customer experience transformations
Strong enterprise transformation background, with experience beyond traditional project management
Deep experience delivering at scale using Agile and SAFe ways of working
Comfortable working in regulated or complex environments, with financial services or digital‑first experience highly regarded
Experienced in blended delivery models, partnering with internal teams, vendors and system integrators
Hands‑on leader able to move seamlessly between strategic oversight and on‑the‑ground execution
Confident traversing business process change, customer experience and technical delivery end‑to‑end
Highly effective stakeholder manager, able to influence senior leaders and navigate complexity
Brings strong commercial judgement, risk awareness and dependency management capability
Clear communicator who simplifies complexity and builds trust across business and technology
A resilient, outcome‑focused change leader with a growth mindset who brings people through transformation, not just platforms
Additional Information

What's on offer

BOSS Best Places to Work 2024 –credited for its culture, engagement and flexible working arrangements. 
Most Inclusive Workplace 2024 – Australian HR Institute (AHRI) Awards. 
Employer of Choice Public Sector and NFP – Australian HR Awards 2023 
Bronze AWEI Accreditation 2024 – Recognised for LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion. 
Accredited Family Friendly Workplace – Supporting work-life balance and inclusivity. 
Hybrid working – Flexible arrangements with state-of-the-art offices designed for collaboration and wellbeing. 
Inclusive leave options – Flexible public holidays, 20 weeks paid parental leave, gender affirmation leave, women’s health leave, and paid time off over Christmas. 
Financial benefits – Not-for-profit salary packaging to boost take-home pay. 
Locations – A team of over 1,500 dedicated professionals based in modern Melbourne and Sydney CBD offices. 

To apply

If you’re passionate about fairness and believe your skills align with this role, we encourage you to apply—even if you don’t meet every single criterion. 

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and gender identities. If you require any accessibility support during the recruitment process, please reach out to our team at Careers@afca.org.au. 

We believe fairness starts with people. That’s why we don’t use AI or automated tools to screen candidates. As a result, our processes may take a little longer, and we thank you for your patience. 

AFCA is a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we are committed to responding to every applicant.

Continuous Improvement Analyst – Customer Service (Contract)

We’re partnering with a well-regarded organisation seeking an experienced professional to step in on a short-term basis and review one of their core customer service functions.

This role will suit someone who enjoys getting into the detail, understanding how teams operate day-to-day, and identifying practical ways to improve efficiency, workflow and overall performance.

The Opportunity

You’ll be engaged to take a fresh, objective look at how the current function is operating, with a focus on identifying opportunities to streamline processes and improve scalability.

Working closely with key stakeholders, you’ll assess current workflows, workload distribution and team capacity, before delivering clear, implementable recommendations.

Key Responsibilities

Map current customer service workflows end-to-end

Assess workload, capacity and work distribution across the team

Identify gaps, duplication and operational inefficiencies

Engage with stakeholders to understand pain points and opportunities

Apply a continuous improvement / Lean lens to current processes

Deliver practical, actionable recommendations to improve performance and scalability

About You

Experience in process improvement, business analysis or operational reviews

Strong ability to map and analyse workflows in detail

Comfortable engaging with stakeholders at all levels

Able to identify inefficiencies and translate findings into practical solutions

Exposure to Lean / continuous improvement methodologies is highly regarded

Background in customer service, operations or support environments will be beneficial

The Details

Immediate start preferred

Contract role (initial short-term engagement)

Flexible working arrangements available

This is a great opportunity to make a tangible impact in a short period of time, with a clear scope and strong stakeholder engagement.

Company: Sirius Technology Sydney part of Sirius People Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer | Microsoft Fabric Specialist
Location: Sydney, NSW (Preferrable) but can be Melbourne, VIC based too.
Role Type: Full-time, Permanent role

We are representing a leading Australian specialist firm that delivers high-impact financial performance and data analytics solutions across the Asia-Pacific region. Our client is looking for a Senior Data Engineer to lead the charge in designing and implementing modern data solutions using Microsoft Fabric.

The Opportunity
In this role, you will bridge the gap between complex business requirements and technical execution. You will architect end-to-end data pipelines, mentor junior engineers, and drive innovation within a highly collaborative Agile environment.

Key Responsibilities
Architect & Design: Lead the implementation of scalable data warehousing, lakehouse, and analytics solutions using Microsoft Fabric.
Develop Pipelines: Build and maintain robust data transformation processes using Python and PySpark.
Data Quality: Design monitoring frameworks to ensure the highest standards of data accuracy and reliability.
Automation & DevOps: Optimize existing pipelines and integrate CI/CD practices to streamline delivery.
Mentorship: Act as a technical lead, guiding junior team members and contributing to strategic architectural decisions.


Technical Requirements
Experience: 5+ years in Data Engineering roles.
Microsoft Fabric Expertise: Deep hands-on experience with Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, and Power BI.
Programming: Advanced Python skills (pandas, numpy, pyspark) and expert-level SQL.
Azure Ecosystem: Proven experience with Azure Synapse Analytics, including SQL and Spark pools.
Frameworks: Strong understanding of dimensional modeling and Agile/Scrum methodologies.
Preferred: Knowledge of Databricks, Delta Lake, or Generative AI/LLM integration.
Why Join This Team?
Our client prides itself on a collegiate culture that balances professional excellence with an enjoyable, supportive work environment.

Flexible Work: Enjoy flexible arrangements to maintain a healthy work-life balance.
Professional Growth: Access to ongoing learning, development opportunities, and exposure to cutting-edge Microsoft technologies.
Impact: Work on some of the largest and most successful IT projects in Australia for a diverse range of industry sectors.
If you are a passionate  Senior Data Engineer with experience on MS stack including MS Fabric beyond proof of concept, we encourage you to apply today via the link provided! Please note Sydney and Melbourne-based candidates with full time work rights only will be considered for this role as it is a requirement for this position and no sponsorship is on offer.

Candidates from all backgrounds are welcome to apply too. Please feel free to also send a CV to kgonzalez@siriustechnology.com.au, our Principal IT Consultant for Data, AI and Cyber, thanks.

This role suits a BA who thrives at the intersection of data, stakeholders, and storytelling, translating complex business needs into clear, actionable reporting outcomes.

What You’ll Be Doing

Lead requirements gathering across diverse stakeholder groups (technical, corporate, and faculty)
Translate business needs into clear dashboard and reporting specifications
Define user stories, acceptance criteria, and KPIs
Partner closely with technical teams to ensure data solutions are fit‑for‑purpose
Support UAT, adoption, and stakeholder buy‑in

Excellent stakeholder management and facilitation skills
Ability to bridge business and technical conversations with confidence

We are seeking an experienced and technically proficient Group Reporting Lead to join a fast-paced, ASX-listed environment on a 6-month contract. This organization is a market leader in essential services with a multi-billion dollar turnover and a consistent track record of growth through acquisition.
This is a hands-on, high-impact role within a complex, multi-entity organisation. You will be responsible for navigating complex consolidations, driving financial governance, and building out the reporting framework. This position is suited for a proactive accountant who can step in immediately and add value through technical precision and process maturity.
 
Key Responsibilities

Consolidations: Manage complex consolidation processes across multiple entities.
Statutory Reporting: Oversee the preparation of statutory accounts and coordinate external audit requirements.
Governance & Policy: Develop and formalize group accounting policies and procedures, creating robust frameworks where documentation is currently limited.
Technical Advisory: Provide high-level technical accounting advice and draft position papers for complex transactions.
Process Improvement: Drive continuous improvement across the group reporting function, focusing on efficiency, automation, and financial control.
Stakeholder Management: Partner with divisional stakeholders to review monthly balance sheet and cash flow submissions.
About You
Qualifications: CA or CPA qualified.
Technical Expertise: Proven experience in group reporting and complex consolidations within a large, multi-entity environment.
Framework Building: Demonstrated ability to build processes, templates, and manuals from the ground up.
Technical Accounting: Strong ability to independently interpret and apply current accounting standards.
Mindset: Commercially astute, solutions-focused, and comfortable working in a high-visibility, fast-moving environment.
Systems: Tech-savvy with an interest in leveraging automation to optimize reporting cycles.
Background: Prior industry experience is essential; exposure to M&A accounting is highly regarded.
Why This Role?
Technical Autonomy: Direct mandate to overhaul reporting frameworks, this isn’t a "business as usual" role; it’s an opportunity to leave a lasting footprint on the group’s financial governance.
Complexity & Stability: Exposure to a sophisticated ASX-listed environment that offers market-leading stability due to its presence in essential services.
Flexibility: Work within a culture that values output over desk-time, supported by flexible hybrid working arrangements.
Social Impact: Join an organization with a mature ESG framework and a genuine commitment to community impact across Australasia.
Senior Exposure: High-visibility role working directly with senior finance leadership on ongoing M&A activity and complex transactions.
Interested? If you are immediately available and possess the required technical expertise, please submit your CV for consideration.

Who is Shift?
At Shift, we’re business specialists dedicated to helping Australian SMEs take control of their cashflow, streamline trade terms and choose the right financial products.

We believe Australian businesses are the driving force behind our economy and are core to our communities. That’s why our business expertise, focus on relationships, and market-leading technology is at the core of everything we do. We’ve helped solve the credit and payment pain points for more than 25,000 businesses, providing over $3.5 billion in aggregate funding.

Our unique approach to product innovation combined with our collaborative culture means you can build your career in a supportive environment. You’ll be joining a diverse team of over 250 people who are always looking to deliver better outcomes for Australian businesses.

About the role:
As a Credit Analyst, you’ll play a key role in the customer journey by assessing credit applications from customers and brokers. Using a mix of bank statements, financial statements and other data, you’ll determine creditworthiness and recommend appropriate lending solutions. Your insights will help shape great customer outcomes while managing risk effectively.

What you'll do:

Assess *commercial credit applications by analysing financial statements, bank statements and other relevant data

Conduct phone interviews with applicants and brokers to gather information and negotiate loan criteria

Provide accurate, quantitative credit assessments and recommend lending limits aligned with Shift’s credit appetite

Ensure correct products and services are applied to customer applications

Maintain detailed records of key risk factors and mitigation strategies to support decision making

Build strong relationships with internal teams, customers, and brokers to enhance business performance

Adhere to credit underwriting standards, compliance policies, and ensure data integrity in core systems

Support Relationship Managers and Sales teams by providing coaching and upskilling

Identify opportunities for process and service improvements to boost team efficiency

What you'll bring:

Previous experience holding a Delegated Lending Authority for commercial/business lending

Strong business acumen and solid understanding of the SME market

Proven track record in delivering excellent customer service

Experience assessing commercial credit using financial and bank statements; experience with non-traditional data is a plus

Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail

Experience in risk or analytics roles or working with finance products in point-of-sale environments is advantageous but not essential

Excellent communication and relationship-building skills

Commitment to compliance and data accuracy

*Please note: Prior experience in commercial/business lending is essential

Key benefits:

Collaborative teams – a flat structure means everyone can learn from colleagues and senior leaders around the business.

Be involved – come together with all of your colleagues every 100 days to share the product and technology roadmap and business strategy.

Flexible working environment – we’re headquartered in North Sydney with state-based workplaces and offer a flexible work policy.

Family support – industry leading 26 weeks paid parental leave.

Varied workspaces – our office enables areas for collaboration, brainstorming and socialising as well as focus zones.

Range of benefits supporting your physical, psychological and financial wellbeing. From a day off on your birthday to excellent end of trip facilities.

GT Motive

GT Motive is the technology division of the Solvd Group & Allianz Partners, delivering innovative estimating, claims and workflow solutions used by insurers and repairers in more than 25 countries. Our mission is to provide open, data driven tools that increase accuracy, streamline operational workflows and improve the overall claims and repair experience.

About the role

The Product Training and Implementation Manager is responsible for driving successful product adoption, integration and ongoing optimisation of GT Motive solutions across Australian insurers and strategic partners, including BMS providers.

This role acts as the primary product interface for the Australian market, translating local requirements into structured input for the global Product and Data teams in Spain, while ensuring customers are fully trained, enabled and supported to maximise value.

Key responsibilities

Product Enablement & Training

Design and deliver structured product training programs for insurer users
Localise onboarding materials, user guides and training documentation
Facilitate virtual and in‑person workshops for rollouts and enhancements
Identify adoption gaps and deploy targeted training interventions
Act as a subject matter expert for insurers and partners

Client Integration & Technical Coordination

Gather business and technical requirements from customers
Translate requirements into structured briefs for global Product and IT teams
Coordinate testing, validation and go‑live activities
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) cycles and integration programs

Market Product Advocacy (Australia)

Represent Australian market needs within global product discussions
Identify market‑specific opportunities and product gaps
Provide structured input into global roadmap prioritisation
Contribute evidence‑based business cases for AU requirements
Monitor competitor activity and industry trends

Ongoing Support & Continuous Improvement

Act as escalation point for complex or recurring product queries
Analyse feedback and propose structural improvements
Monitor product performance and user adoption
Support communication of product updates and releases
Ensure alignment between product capability and insurer workflows

What you’ll bring

Essential:

Experience in automotive, insurance or claims ecosystems
Background in product delivery, system integration or client implementation
Strong analytical and structured thinking capability
Ability to translate business needs into technical requirements
Demonstrated experience delivering structured training programs

Desirable:

Experience working in a global or matrixed organisation
Familiarity with product lifecycle processes
Experience contributing to product roadmaps or requirement definition

Herron Todd White (HTW) is Australia’s largest independent property valuation and advisory firm. With over 55 years of experience and a new partnership with Quadrant Private Equity, we’re entering an exciting growth phase. 

Our 800+ professionals nationwide combine local expertise with national scale to deliver trusted insights that help clients make confident property decisions.

We are seeking a Senior Technology Business Analyst who thrives in a complex, multi-system environment and brings discipline to both requirements and delivery across Herron Todd White.

The Role

Based in our Melbourne office, this is an exciting permanent opportunity, contributing in our Technology Delivery & Change function as a Senior Technology Business Analyst. Operating as a practice leader, and an individual contributor, you will shape and embed within a delivery squad to perform core BA duties reporting to the Head of Technology Delivery. 

Key Responsibilities

Discovery & Requirements: Lead elicitation and discovery for complex initiatives involving multi-system dependencies and major regulatory changes.

Solution Design: Partner with engineering and architecture teams to evaluate technical feasibility and produce functional specifications and data mapping.

Agile Leadership: Maintain backlog management practices and champion the quality of Agile ceremonies (Scrum/Kanban) across squads.

Delivery Support: Work with Squad Leads and Product Management on prioritization, sprint planning, and UAT coordination.

Practice Uplift: Mentor peer BAs and uplift team standards, templates, and documentation quality.

Skills & Experience

Experience: 6+ years as a Business Analyst in a technology delivery environment.

Technical Depth: Proven ability to lead discovery for complex, cross-platform, or multi-system initiatives.

Agile Proficiency: Deep experience with backlog management, user story decomposition, and Jira/Confluence.

Stakeholder Management: Proven ability to challenge, engage, and align stakeholders across all levels — from delivery squads through to executive leadership.

Qualifications: Tertiary degree in IT or Business. Certifications like CBAP, CCBA, CSPO, or SAFe are highly desirable.

This role is well suited to a Senior BA who thrives navigating in changing environments, leads critical initiatives, and delivers with the mindset to uplift standards.

Title:  Manager, Strategic Insights & Engagement


Victoria, Australia

Employment Type:  Fixed Term Full Time

12 month Fixed Term contract

We bring the energy! 

Through purpose and people - not just through the infrastructure we operate, but through the way we show up for each other, our customers’ and our communities. The future of energy is in our hands. Let’s shape it together. 

At AusNet, what we do matters — and so does how we listen. As we continue Australia’s energy transition, we’re looking for a Manager, Strategic Engagement & Insights to lead how we connect with customers, communities and stakeholders, and translate those insights into better decisions and outcomes.

This is a highly visible leadership role with real influence across the organisation, supporting major regulatory programs and long‑term network planning across electricity transmission, electricity distribution and gas.

About the role

Reporting to the General Manager, Public Affairs, you’ll lead a small, high‑performing team responsible for stakeholder engagement, customer research and insight generation across AusNet.

You’ll work across the business to ensure customer and stakeholder perspectives are embedded into strategy, planning and proposals – playing a key role in shaping AusNet’s reputation, regulatory outcomes and transition to net zero.

What you’ll be doing

Leading and developing a team of engagement and research specialists.
Designing and overseeing high‑quality engagement processes linked to major business planning initiatives (including price reviews).
Overseeing AusNet’s customer research program, including customer satisfaction and energy sentiment tracking.
Managing key customer and stakeholder forums and advisory groups.
Acting as a trusted adviser to leaders across the business on engagement strategy and customer insights.
Translating engagement and research outcomes into clear, actionable business impact.
Strengthening AusNet’s customer‑centric approach to planning, decision‑making and delivery.
Building and maintaining strong relationships with customers, advocates, industry, regulators and government.
Promoting AusNet’s work externally, including through the annual Energy Charter Disclosure reporting and stakeholder newsletter.

What we’re looking for: 

Strong experience leading stakeholder engagement and/or customer insight functions in complex environments.
Confidence and experience engaging effectively with senior leaders, external stakeholders and diverse audiences.
Excellent judgement, communication and relationship‑building skills.
Ability to translate complex technical or regulatory concepts into clear, accessible engagement materials.
Experience leading people and managing competing priorities.
Exposure to the energy sector, regulation or price reviews is highly regarded, but not essential.

The AusNet experience is all about…  

Solving some of the most complex energy challenges of our time: We love unpacking complex challenges in clever ways and looking at things differently.  
When life happens, knowing we’ve got your back: Whether it's life's big moments or the unexpected little ones, we get it – sometimes you just need to be there.  
Being part of a team that cares deeply about each other: We share in the good times. We support in the tough times. And we’re humble through and through.  

We’re committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce. We believe different perspectives are essential to our success, and we encourage all applicants to apply. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, we're happy to discuss how we can support you.

Job Title: Professor, Flinders Factory of the Future
Ad Text:
Position Details

Continuing | Full-Time
Academic Level E | AU$217,876 pa | 17% superannuation
Location: Tonsley / Kaurna Country
View Candidate Brochure


About the Position:
The Flinders Factory of the Future is a transdisciplinary research and innovation hub designed to accelerate industry growth through practical, evidence-based solutions. Bringing together expertise across business, public policy, engineering, and science, the Factory partners directly with industry to solve real-world challenges and build capability for the future workforce.

We are seeking an internationally recognised academic leader to join the initiative as Professor, providing strategic leadership in research, teaching, and industry engagement across one of the following areas:

Economics of Advanced Manufacturing, productivity, and public policy
Additive Manufacturing with a business lens, for example, human factors and business models.
Artificial Intelligence in the workplace, business models and regulation.

The Professor will strengthen the University's national and international reputation in one of the above disciplines through high-impact research, education, and strategic partnerships.


About You
You are an internationally recognised academic leader with a distinguished record of research excellence, teaching leadership, and impactful engagement with industry and external stakeholders.

To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate:

A PhD or equivalent qualification in a relevant discipline
A sustained and distinguished track record of high-quality research outputs and competitive grant success (Category 1 or equivalent)
Significant experience leading curriculum development, teaching programs, and educational innovation at tertiary level
A strong record of HDR supervision and successful student completions at doctoral level
Demonstrated leadership in building and sustaining research collaborations with national and international partners, including industry engagement
Proven ability to provide academic leadership and mentorship to researchers and academic teams

This position is open to international candidates and relocation and visa support is available.


Life at Flinders
We're transforming and investing in people and facilities to create contemporary, stimulating, and satisfying learning and work environments that reflect our core values of excellence, innovation, courage, and integrity. Flinders is refocusing its strategic priorities with the aim of elevating its performance to be a top ten Australian university, and amongst the top 1% in the world.

Aligned with Flinders University's Impact. With Purpose. strategy, this role will drive meaningful impact through industry-engaged research, innovative teaching, and collaboration that addresses real-world challenges and shapes the future workforce.

So, why work at Flinders?

17% Superannuation + salary packaging options
Flexible working arrangements
Ranked in the top 2% of universities worldwide.
Ninety per cent of Flinders research has been ranked at 'world standard or above' by Excellence in Research for Australia (ERA).
Wide range of professional development activities and services, such as the Flinders University's Researcher Development and Leadership Programs
Wide range of professional development activities and services
Vibrant campus life and amenities including on campus health care services, gym and childcare centre (Bedford Park, South Australia).


Our Commitment to Reconciliation and Indigenous Employment
Flinders University is proud to be an organisation that is committed to our Reconciliation Action Plan and Indigenous Workforce Strategy. Our vision is to be a preferred employer for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. We are committed to progressing Indigenous advancement in education, research, employment, and wellbeing, and strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples for all Flinders vacancies.

Artificial Intelligence in the workplace, business models and regulation.

The role:
Reporting to the Associate Director of Design & Delivery, the Change Delivery Manager will support the Claims & Assessing leadership team through the management of change initiatives, utilising project, product delivery and change management principles as well as providing advice and support. You will lead a team of Business Analysts, Change Analysts and Program Managers to deliver Claims and Assessing solutions that maintain alignment to the C&A strategy that achieves business outcomes and improve the customer and consultant experience. 

Position Accountabilities:

Lead the end-to-end delivery of initiatives by defining clear scope, objectives, and quality outcomes. This includes translating business requirements into actionable features, collaborating with technology teams for solution design, and ensuring all deliverables meet project goals and achieve agreed business value.
Proactively manage stakeholder relationships at all levels, fostering strong partnerships across business and technology.
Maintain transparent communication, effectively manage escalations, and implement robust change management strategies to engage impacted teams and align initiatives with business strategy.
Collaborate with leadership and portfolio groups to effectively prioritise and resource the portfolio of work, managing the change backlog and ensuring alignment with broader strategic and financial drivers. This also includes working with technology teams to prioritise and schedule the resolution of production defects.
Coach and mentor team members, driving continuous improvement through active participation in agile ceremonies and knowledge sharing.
Foster the development of subject matter expertise, make rapid tactical decisions, and conduct post-implementation monitoring and reviews to optimise benefits realisation and disseminate key learnings.
Lead and manage the Change Delivery Team effectively, driving high performance, monitoring KPIs, and ensuring successful project outcomes in alignment with The Auto & General Way.
Foster an inclusive and high-integrity culture, building strong relationships with stakeholders and team members, and developing specialist capabilities to empower successful change implementation across the organisation.


Qualifications & Experience:

Essential

Proven experience in Project, Product, Change, and Delivery Management, including the leadership of matrix teams, with a track record of successful business delivery.
Minimum 3 years of experience in an Agile leadership role, including leading teams within an Agile environment.
Demonstrated ability to effectively engage stakeholders at all levels and across organisational functions.
Superior communication skills, coupled with high-level collaboration abilities, accuracy & attention to detail.
Superior analytical and problem-solving skill, with the ability to quickly learn new systems & processes.

Desirable

A sound understanding of Claims & Assessing operations as it relates to general insurance.
Tertiary qualifications or certifications in Information Technology or a related discipline
Extensive experience in requirements gathering and business analysis, particularly for system enhancements, including leading user acceptance testing.
Experience in managing external suppliers.
A well-developed and demonstrated understanding of the legislative, regulatory, and compliance obligations to operate in the FSR environment.
Exposure to a Contact Centre environment is highly desirable.


The benefits of working at A&G:

Location – North Lakes: This role is based at our North Lakes office. Save the long commute to Brisbane and work for a growing company close to home and within walking distance to some of the north side’s best retail outlets, restaurants and other amenities. This is an on-site role.
Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.
Perks App - Access to an employee benefits and discounts app called ‘Perks’ offering your great discounts, offers and programs across a range of areas

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community.  

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Virgin Money and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.

Job Description

The Senior Agile Coach will play a pivotal part in shaping how AFCA delivers. You’ll lead the embedment of scaled agile ways of working across digital, technology and operational teams, crucial to bringing momentum, consistency and confidence to delivery.

This is a hands‑on role combining coaching, change leadership and practical execution. From working side‑by‑side with teams to designing fit‑for‑purpose Jira workflows, you’ll lift delivery flow, predictability and outcomes in a complex, highly regulated environment undergoing significant transformation.

This is scoped as 2 year max term contract. 

What you’ll do

Lead and embed Scaled Agile (SAFe) ways of working across portfolios, programs and teams
Facilitate organisation‑wide ceremonies such as Big Room Planning and Quarterly PI Planning
Act as a hands‑on delivery coach, working directly with squads, ARTs and leaders to uplift execution and effectiveness
Design, implement and optimise Jira workflows, boards, reporting and delivery metrics
Build and mature cross‑functional teams across digital, technology and operational areas
Coach Product Owners, Scrum Masters, Delivery Leads and senior leaders on agile roles, ceremonies and accountabilities
Support PI planning, dependency management, sprint ceremonies and continuous improvement practices
Enable effective delivery across blended in‑house and vendor teams, aligned to agreed ways of working
Foster a culture of transparency, continuous improvement and outcome‑focused delivery
Lead with confidence in fast‑paced, complex and high‑transformation environments
Qualifications

What you'll bring: 

Proven experience leading Scaled Agile implementations at scale.  
Strong background in hands‑on agile delivery, not purely advisory or coaching roles.  
Deep, practical experience implementing and configuring Jira for agile and scaled agile delivery.  
Demonstrated experience working across digital, technology and operational teams.  
Experience operating in highly regulated environments (financial services, government or similar strongly preferred).  
Experience working with vendor and partner‑led delivery models.  
SAFe certification (e.g. SPC, SAFe Agile Coach) highly desirable. 
Pragmatic, delivery‑focused and comfortable rolling up sleeves.  
Strong change leadership and coaching capability at team and senior leadership levels.  
Excellent stakeholder engagement and influencing skills.  
Growth mindset with a can‑do attitude and resilience in high‑change environments.  
Clear, confident communicator able to translate agile principles into practical behaviours.

Locations – A team of over 1,500 dedicated professionals based in modern Melbourne and Sydney CBD offices.

Job Title: Senior Data Architect - 3 month Contract - $900 per day
Ad Text:
Senior Data Architect - Melbourne - $900 per Day - 3 Month Contract with a view to a permanent role - Own Data Strategy and Design - Immediate Start

 

Morgan Consulting are partnering with a leading organisation to appoint a Senior Data Architect on an initial 3 month contract with a view to a permanent role, paying $900 per day, with an immediate start available. This is a high impact role where you will own data architecture strategy and design, working within a mature Data Product team to support large scale, business critical data platforms. You will play a key role in shaping modern, cloud based data solutions within a fast moving, delivery focused environment.

 

About the Role

The Senior Data Architect is responsible for defining and driving the data architecture vision, strategy, and design across enterprise data platforms. Working closely with Data Engineers, Data Scientists and Product Managers, you will ensure data solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned to both technical and commercial objectives. This role is hands on and delivery focused, combining strategic architecture leadership with deep technical expertise across modern data engineering practices. You will operate within a Data Product team, helping translate complex business needs into clear, well designed data solutions.


What You Will Do

Data Architecture and Strategy


Own and define the data architecture strategy and target state designs across cloud based data platforms
Design scalable, secure, and high performing data architectures aligned to business and product outcomes
Provide architectural guidance across ingestion, transformation, storage, and consumption layers
Ensure architectural standards, patterns, and governance frameworks are clearly defined and applied

Data Engineering and Platforms


Work closely with Data Engineers to design and support ETL and ELT pipelines and data integration patterns
Contribute hands on expertise across Azure data services, Databricks, and modern cloud data tooling
Support CI CD practices for data platforms, pipelines, and infrastructure as code
Ensure data solutions are production ready, resilient, and optimised for performance and cost

Stakeholder and Product Collaboration


Partner with Data Product Managers and business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data designs
Provide clear architectural direction and communicate complex concepts in a pragmatic, outcome focused way
Act as a trusted advisor across technology and data teams

What You Will Bring

Proven experience as a Senior Data Architect or Lead Data Architect within complex environments
Strong background in data engineering alongside data architecture
Deep experience with Azure, including modern data services and cloud native architectures
Hands on expertise with ETL and ELT, CI CD, and Databricks
Strong understanding of modern data product operating models
Experience working in retail or customer centric environments highly regarded
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to operate autonomously and deliver impact quickly in a contract setting
 

Why Join

$900 per day with immediate start
3 month contract with meaningful impact from day one
Opportunity to own data architecture strategy and design in a modern data environment
Work within a mature Data Product team using modern cloud and engineering practices
Fast paced, delivery focused culture with minimal red tape
 
If you are a Senior Data Architect with a strong data engineering background who thrives in fast paced environments and enjoys owning strategy as well as delivery, we would love to hear from you.
Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

About the Role

Providing senior level leadership and coaching to project teams and other project managers to ensure information technology solutions are delivered and implemented in an effective and sustainable manner, the role is responsible for leading, managing and successfully delivering information technology related projects, using fit for purpose approaches and methodologies (e.g. waterfall, agile or hybrid) that deliver business value and outcomes in an effective and sustainable manner.

Some of the key responsibilities of the position include:

Leading on the planning, executing and reporting on multiple and concurrent projects, delivering complex information technology solutions with enterprise wide impacts.

Experience working on customer-facing digital platforms

Familiarity with emerging technologies and digital ecosystems

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills

About the role

The Credit & Affordability team is seeking a Commercial Strategy & Insights Manager to join the Melbourne-based team on a permanent basis. Reporting to the Head of Credit, this role is responsible for delivering commercial insights, financial performance management, and end‑to‑end collections reporting across operational spend and outcomes. The role also oversees the delivery and optimisation of technical platforms and assets, ensuring continuity and alignment across processes, practices, and technology teams.

The role supports delivery of AGL's strategic objectives through effective governance and performance management of credit and collections partners, alongside robust insights and reporting to manage Credit & Affordability OPEX and NBDE targets (P&L).

Leveraging data and analytics, this role analyses collections journeys and bad-debt drivers to develop and implement evidence‑based resolution strategies. This includes optimising collection methodologies, control sizes, treatment durations, and customer segmentation to drive best‑in‑class performance, maximise debt coverage, minimise debtor volumes, and uphold compliance and adherence standards.


What you'll be doing:

Deliver financial forecasts and budgeting against OPEX for Credit and Affordability (C&A) as well as Governance of monthly reporting and reconciliations ensuring budget is well maintained and not exceeded.
A proven ability to manage commercial debt collection outcomes while enhancing customer experience
Deliver management reporting to track and measure the effectiveness of each program of work in line with financial targets. Perform regular performance and audit reviews, holding partners accountable to develop action plans and strategies to improve results and add value to the AGL business.
Set partnership and insights strategies that support the overall Credit and Affordability goals and targets
Partner with Data Platform and Data Engineering teams to embed governance principles into data platforms and workflows.
Develop management strategies to transition analytical data assets into enterprise-wide solutions for broader adoption.
Leverage AI to drive deeper insights and improve team performance.


What you'll bring:

Demonstrates a high level of independence and accountability, confidently managing complex enquiries, escalations, and sensitive internal and external matters
Leads the translation of operational strategy into scalable, customer‑centric delivery models across multiple layers of the organisation
Exhibits strong conceptual and strategic thinking, with the ability to navigate complexity and influence long‑term outcomes
Brings strong commercial acumen, balancing financial performance, customer outcomes, and risk to drive sustainable results
Applies disciplined risk management through robust systems, governance frameworks, and control mechanisms
Provides inspirational leadership through effective coaching, performance management, and capability development, building high‑performing and engaged teams
