Job Title: Lead Product Manager – Melbourne – $200,000 - Gain AI – Amazing Culture
Ad Text:
Lead Product Manager – Melbourne – $200,000 - Lead a Product Team – Productionised AI Features – Strong Career Development – Amazing Culture

Morgan Consulting are partnering with an exciting and forward thinking organisation to appoint a Lead Product Manager to lead a high performing product team and drive the strategy, vision, and execution across a critical suite of business critical applications. This is a standout opportunity to deliver AI enabled, production ready features, influence enterprise‑scale products, and shape both customer and operational experiences. This role offers strong career development within an organisation that empowers its people, moves quickly, and fosters a collaborative, inclusive, and high trust culture with minimal red tape.

We are looking for a true product specialist who is equally comfortable with the data and engineering foundations of modern products as they are with the craft of product management itself. Someone who brings energy, curiosity, and genuine passion for product, and who thrives on turning complexity into clear, valuable outcomes. You will have strong stakeholder management skills, able to confidently balance maintaining and improving existing products while also driving the introduction of new capabilities, including AI enabled features, to deliver meaningful impact.

About the Role

The Lead Product Manager is responsible for leading and developing a team of six Product Specialists while owning the strategic direction, vision, and delivery of a portfolio of applications that underpin core operations. This role blends people leadership with hands on product strategy, working closely with engineering, data, design, and business stakeholders to deliver high impact, customer‑centred solutions. A key focus of the role is supporting the delivery of AI driven and productionised features, ensuring products are scalable, reliable, and aligned with both customer needs and commercial outcomes. You will play a pivotal role in fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and outcome driven delivery across the product organisation.

What You Will Do

Product Strategy & Delivery
Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap across a suite of applications supporting large scale operations.
Guide prioritisation decisions to balance customer value, commercial outcomes, and technical sustainability.
Lead the delivery of AI enabled and data driven features, ensuring solutions are production ready and scalable.
Partner closely with engineering teams to ensure products are built, operated, and continuously improved using modern development practices.
Drive strong alignment between business objectives and product outcomes.
Leadership & Team Development
Lead, mentor, and inspire a team of six Product Managers, supporting their growth and capability development.
Foster a high performance culture centred on accountability, psychological safety, and continuous improvement.
Set clear expectations and provide coaching on product discovery, delivery, and storytelling.
Stakeholder & Customer Engagement
Act as a trusted product leader across the business, engaging stakeholders at all levels.
Communicate product vision, strategy, and progress through clear narratives and compelling storytelling.
Collaborate with internal users and customers to ensure solutions deliver meaningful and measurable value.
Innovation & Ways of Working
Champion experimentation, learning, and innovation across the product function.
Promote modern product management practices, including outcome based roadmaps and evidence driven decision making.
Work collaboratively across product, engineering, data, and design to deliver seamless, end to end experiences.
What You Will Bring
Proven experience in leading a product management team, owning complex digital products and platforms.
Demonstrated experience leading and developing high performing product teams.
Strong understanding of how modern applications are designed, built, operated, and maintained.
Hands on experience working directly alongside software engineers and technology teams.
Exceptional communication, stakeholder engagement, and storytelling skills.
Ability to balance strategic leadership with pragmatic, hands‑on execution.
Preferred Experience
Experience working in large‑scale, multi‑system or customer centric environments.
Familiarity with agile delivery, modern product operating models, and scaled product organisations.
Why Join
Opportunity to lead and shape production ready AI powered products at scale.
Strong career development and leadership progression opportunities.
Inclusive, people first culture where individuals are empowered to make a real impact.
Commitment to diversity, fairness, and equal opportunity across all backgrounds and life stages.
If you are a Lead Product Manager who blends deep product craft with a strong understanding of data and engineering, thrives in fast paced environments, enjoys leading and growing high‑performing teams, and is driven to deliver impactful products that matter, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

Ad Text:
Ding! Ding! Trams are the heartbeat of Melbourne - come be part of our team on the largest and most iconic tram network in the world.


About the Opportunity

Yarra Trams is seeking a Data Platform Specialist to drive the technical governance, engineering standards, and data management practices that underpin our enterprise Data Management Platform (DMP). This role plays a key part in ensuring that the organisation's data assets are secure, high-quality, and shared responsibly across the business and with the Department of Transport.
You will define reusable engineering patterns, uplift governance maturity, and guide the consistent use of Azure Synapse, Databricks, ADF and related technologies. You will also lead the development and coordination of data governance frameworks, support information management activities, and partner with stakeholders to improve enterprise data quality and reliability.


What You Will Be Doing

Data & Information Management

Implement and maintain the Data and Information Management Plan (DIMP).
Develop data governance frameworks, policies, standards and procedures.
Coordinate enterprise data sharing and information management processes.
Conduct maturity assessments, audits and gap analysis to support continuous improvement.
Maintain the Yarra Trams information asset register, metadata, and associated data models.
Support change management and uplift in data governance capability across the organisation.

Safety, Risk and Compliance

Support threat identification and risk mitigation for data and information assets.
Ensure privacy‑by‑design and security‑by‑design principles are applied across the DMP.

Stakeholder Engagement & Technical Leadership

Provide guidance and training to uplift data governance capability.
Collaborate with the Data and Analytics team to maintain data integrity.
Work closely with Data Owners, Custodians and the Department of Transport to align data standards.
Support adoption of consistent engineering patterns across Azure Synapse, Databricks and ADF.

What You Will Bring

2+ years' experience in Data Governance, Data Architecture or Data Analytics.
Strong understanding of Azure data environments, SQL and BI systems.
Experience implementing data governance frameworks and technical standards.
Experience with Azure data services including Synapse, ADF, Databricks and data modelling.
Familiarity with DAMA-DMBOK or similar frameworks.
Strong analytical skills and ability to interpret governance or compliance requirements.
Ability to influence stakeholders across technical and operational domains.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Applications close: 26 April 2026

Do you want to be part of a workplace that promotes respect, diversity and inclusion?

If you choose to work with us, you will be joining a team of passionate professionals dedicated to the delivery and improvement of Melbourne's biggest icon!

Competitive salary and great conditions

Senior Strategic Analyst

Full time, permanent role.
Geelong office location, with flexible hybrid working
Offering a competitive base salary of circa $149,000 plus super and benefits

Care in Action
With a career at the TAC, you’ll be helping us look out for the lives of everyone who travels on Victoria’s roads. That’s care in action.

Find out more about our shared purpose, and what drives every one of us.

Right now, we’re seeking a Senior Strategic Analyst who will play their part in this shared purpose, while also supporting the progress of clients and colleagues so they can achieve great things. That’s truly rewarding work that will help us continue to deliver life-critical services where it matters. 

About the role
This role is focused on delivering strategic performance insights and supporting the implementation of our “Make Every Day Matter” strategy.

With our strategy mid-cycle, you’ll partner with Executive and Senior leaders to identify trends, generate insights and support informed decision-making, translating complex analysis into clear strategic narratives.

 

About the Team
You’ll be one of three Senior Strategic Analysts, working as part of the broader Strategy team within the Performance, Risk and Strategy division. This is a highly collaborative, team-focused environment where you will work closely together, value open communication, and support each other to deliver high-quality outcomes.

 

What we’re looking for

Strategic performance & analytical expertise
You bring strong experience in analysing both quantitative and qualitative data, identifying trends and translating insights into meaningful performance outcomes. You’re confident working with data and visualising it in a way that drives action.

Influential communicator
You have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex analysis into clear, compelling insights. You’re comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders, including executive and Board-level audiences, and can influence through evidence-based storytelling.

Collaborative team player
You thrive in a team environment, working closely with others to solve problems and deliver outcomes. You value collaboration over autonomy and enjoy contributing to a shared goal, while building strong relationships across the organisation.

 

Why you’ll love it here
There’s more to work than a competitive salary and amazing employee benefits. As our newest Senior Strategic Analyst, you’ll also:

Make a positive difference
Every day the work you do at the TAC contributes to something bigger. As part of an inclusive, supportive team you’ll find real purpose in achieving goals, together.

Be rewarded for great work
We value our people, offering a service delivery payment, salary packaging and a reward and recognition program.

Grow and go places
We’re invested in your progress. When you work for one of Victoria’s largest and leading workplaces, you’ll receive extensive internal opportunities for development and access to the LinkedIn Learning Digital Hub.

Ready to put care in action? We’d like to hear from you.
Click apply to submit your Cover Letter and Resume. If successful, you’ll progress to an interview. All applicants will be advised of the outcome of their application.

For more information, read the attached Position Description or contact Shay McLean on shay_mclean@tac.vic.gov.au for a confidential discussion. Applications for this role will be accepted up until 11:55pm Sunday 10 May 2026.

If we can do anything to help make applying for this role easier, or you have an adjustment request, please let our People, Culture & Engagement Team know at recruitment@tac.vic.gov.au. If over the phone is better for you, our number is 03 9300 0501.

A safe place to work

This extends to inclusion, diversity and accessibility in our employment practices, and encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disability, men, women and gender diverse people, culturally diverse, and LGBTIQA+ people to apply for roles.

Child Safety
At the TAC we are committed to the safety of children and young people. For more information regarding our commitment to child safety, please visit our website.

Job Title: Portfolio Manager - Sustainable Investment Private Markets
Salary: Great salary plus 14% super and bonus potential
Ad Text:
Lead sustainable investing where it counts - private markets. Drive sustainable investment integration and stewardship across infrastructure, partnering with deal teams and external managers to strengthen long‑term outcomes.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As Portfolio Manager – Sustainable Investment Private Markets, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping how sustainability is embedded across Australian Retirement Trust’s private markets portfolio, with a primary focus on infrastructure.


You’ll lead the integration of financially material sustainability risks and opportunities into investment decision‑making, manager selection, asset management and stewardship activities. Working closely with internal deal teams and external managers, you’ll help ensure long‑term, resilient investment outcomes that are firmly in the best financial interests of our members.


This role also contributes to broader private markets asset classes including real estate, private equity and private credit, while supporting ART’s Net Zero Roadmap and broader sustainable investment strategy.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Lead sustainable investment integration across infrastructure, embedding financially material sustainability risks and opportunities into due diligence, manager oversight and asset management.
Partner with investment and deal teams on direct and co‑investment opportunities, assessing how sustainability factors impact financial performance.
Engage external infrastructure managers and assets to drive sustainability‑led value creation aligned with ART’s expectations.
Integrate climate‑related requirements into infrastructure portfolios to strengthen resilience to physical and transition risks and support ART’s Net Zero ambitions.
Contribute to ESG data collection, analysis and insights, particularly across infrastructure, to support decision‑making and improved sustainable investment practices.
Report on the effectiveness of private market integration and stewardship activities, highlighting risks, challenges and recommendations.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Sydney or Brisbane.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You hold relevant tertiary qualifications in finance, investments, commerce, or economics.
You bring relevant experience in a sustainability, responsible investment, financial services, investment management, consulting or business role. Prior experience in infrastructure will be highly regarded.
You’ve got a working knowledge of key sustainable investment topics (climate change, human rights, biodiversity etc.) and SI frameworks (AASB, SASB, PCAF, GRESB, EDCI etc.).
You’ve got proven research and analytical skills, and you know how to use data to drive strategic and creative thinking.
You’re skilled at managing stakeholders and negotiating outcomes that serve the best interests of our members.
You’re a clear and persuasive communicator, able to present confidently to senior management and executive teams.
You’re commercially savvy and comfortable reviewing legal documents.
You know how to juggle competing priorities and stay focused in a fast-paced environment.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Sunday, 17 May 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

About the Role

This Business Analyst role focuses on partnering with business stakeholders to analyse, define and deliver process and system improvements that support strategic objectives. You will work across operational, compliance and technology initiatives, translating complex business needs into clear, actionable requirements and supporting successful change delivery.


Key Responsibilities
Elicit and document business requirements through workshops and interviews
Analyse current-state processes and design future-state improvements
Develop high-quality documentation including process maps and acceptance criteria
Act as a liaison between business stakeholders, delivery teams and vendors
Support testing activities including UAT and validation of outcomes
About You
Proven experience as a Business Analyst on system or financial services projects
Strong skills in requirements gathering, process mapping and documentation
Confident stakeholder engagement and communication skills
High attention to detail with strong analytical capability
Ability to manage multiple deliverables in a project environment
About the Company
Our Client is a well-established, Brisbane-based organisation with a national footprint. They specialise in delivering critical services within a highly regulated environment and are known for their strong governance, collaborative culture and continued investment in technology and process improvement.


For more information or a confidential discussion, please apply.

Company Information

Large global insurance claims management and outsourced solutions business. They are the market leader and continue to grow at a rapid rate. They currently have a highly commercial finance business partner (senior financial analyst) role they are looking to fill with an outstanding candidate.

This business has a highly progressive culture and outstanding staff retention and engagement rate. They are made up of driven people who thrive in the environment and are passionate about what they do. They offer flexible working with 3 days work from home and 2 days in the office. The office is based on the city fringe and is close to all types of public transport.

Responsibilities

This is a business critical, commercial Finance Business Partner role that reports into the Finance Director. The key focus areas of the role are highlighted below -

Work closely with executive management on strategy development and implementation from a commercial finance standpoint

Overseeing the budget process from start to finish. Work closely with all key stakeholders to consolidate information and present the final budget to business leaders

Work closely with senior management and offer value add advice and analysis to support key decision making

Monthly analysis of business unit results

Maintain and update profit and loss forecast

Assist with the tendering process for new business opportunities

Develop and maintain pricing models

Assist with transformation projects in relation to new systems and technology advancements 

The role has one direct report Finance Analyst

Background

Someone from a Senior Finance Analyst/Finance Business Partner/FP&A Analyst or similar role

CPA/CA qualified with experience in budgeting, business partnering, stakeholder engagement, in depth financial analysis and management reporting

Experience with business modelling, pricing proposals, margin analysis, cost allocation etc

Ability to manage and mentor one Analyst

Someone who has presented effectively in meetings (financials/budgets etc)

Insurance or financial services industry experience would be advantageous but open to any industry (if you have the right experience and fit into the team environment)

An energetic, influential and relationship focused candidate with excellent communication skills

The role requires someone who is confident, proactive and enjoys developing relationships with stakeholders at all levels

Benefits on offer

The role is highly commercial, analytical and has a lot of stakeholder engagement 

Opportunity to really influence and make an impact

Growth business that is willing to invest in its people and offer career progression

Great culture with passionate people who thrive in this environment

Leadership role. The role is 2nd in charge to the Finance Director

Excellent salary package and bonus on offer

Large global market leader

Hybrid role with 3 days work from home per week

If you are interested and suitable for this role please apply.

Job Title: Senior Category Development Manager, Customer Lead
Ad Text:
A bit about us  

If you are looking for a fantastic team and a challenging, fast, and dynamic work environment, you are at the right place!  

On our journey to making a positive difference across the world, you will work with passionate people in an innovative and collaborative organization as we inspire wellness through #PurposeLedCareers  

We are dedicated to our customers, community, and environment and are looking for similar-minded talent to join us! As an equal opportunity employer, we care deeply about creating an inclusive workplace where our team members feel valued, respected, and empowered.  

Our total rewards package offering includes additional annual leave, bonuses, wellness programs, health benefits, flexible and remote working arrangements, and performance awards - not to mention various social and recreational activities, all of which are regional specific.  

With over 3,400 team members working across the globe, our vision is focused on premium nutrition and lifetime wellness, backed by science. We have 3 business segments - Baby, Adult and Pet Nutrition and Care - supporting whole - family health and happiness across 7 international brands.




What this means for you 

The variety of work creates unlimited opportunities to excel across a breadth of disciplines; you will be presented with a wealth of new pathways and opportunities to explore throughout your career. Working with us will be more than just a job; we guarantee a life-changing experience as you step into an environment that moves quickly and presents new challenges daily.




Your role within the team 

As the Senior Category Development Manager, Customer Lead, you will play a pivotal part in driving strategic category growth and fostering strong retailer partnerships. This role is responsible for delivering data-driven insights, collaborating closely with sales, marketing, and business management teams to optimise brand and channel plans. You’ll lead the identification of new merchandising opportunities, manage and enhance planograms, and coach team members, ensuring continuous development. Leveraging your experience in FMCG, advanced analytical skills, and ability to influence at all levels, you will help position H&H as the go-to partner for category leadership and innovation.




Key responsibilities include: 

Champion the category, serving as an objective expert for both H&H and our retail partners. 

Drive strong retailer relationships and be the partner of choice for category data and insight. 

Collaborate closely with sales and marketing teams to ensure brand, innovation, and channel strategies are driven by insights. 

Work alongside Business Managers to develop channel, account, and Joint Business Plans (JBP), leading portfolio opportunity analysis for key retail partners. 

Identify and execute opportunities to expand our merchandising footprint, managing and optimising planograms based on data-driven insights. 

Mentor and develop the Category Analyst, fostering continuous growth and capability within the team.




Desired Skills & Experience 

We look for people who have what it takes to make an impact; who can challenge us to go further while pushing themselves to deliver exciting & ambitious results.  

To make sure we’re setting new starters up for success, we ask that you meet a few criteria relevant to the role to be considered for this opportunity: 

Minimum 5 years’ experience in a similar capacity within the FMCG sector. 

Demonstrated capability to analyse both quantitative and qualitative data, transforming them into practical business insights. 

Track record of successfully influencing and collaborating with cross-functional teams at all organisational levels. 

Proven experience in customer-facing roles, particularly working with major retail partners. 

Proficient user of IQVIA and Nielsen data tools; familiarity with Quantium is highly regarded. 

Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal. 

Advanced Microsoft Excel proficiency.




H&H Group believes in the benefits of a diverse & inclusive workplace & aims to reflect the varied cultures within which we exist. We are committed to providing a working environment that is free from discrimination & harassment, creating a safe and inclusive environment for all.  

We encourage applications from people of all ages, nationalities, religions, racial and gender identities, sexual orientations, abilities and cultures, & our hiring decisions will be based on business needs, position requirements, & the qualifications and experience of individuals. If you require support or assistance during the application process, please notify us at the time of application and we will be sure to enable reasonable adjustments where suitable.  

If you have what it takes, please apply via the online portal & we will be in touch soon. We move quickly & may fill the role prior to the application close date, so we suggest you get in touch today!

Job Title: Principal Development and Business Intelligence Officer - Cairns
Company: Cairns & Hinterland Hospital and Health Service
Category:
Business/Systems Analysts (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About us: 

Our values were developed by our staff, patients and community. They guide our work and actions every day. Our staff are part of the community we serve. We believe that we can improve health outcomes by involving our community in the planning and evaluation of their local health services.

The Activity, Funding and Business Intelligence (AFBI) team provide analytics, business intelligence, funding and clinical costing advice for Cairns and Hinterland Hospital Health Services across acute care, ambulatory services and sub and non-acute patient episodes of care.

About your opportunity:

In this position you will….. 

Undertake all work deliverables to the highest standard and ensure all work outputs adhere to internal quality assurance models, frameworks and requirements.
Design and develop comprehensive Business Intelligence (BI) solutions, to support operational and strategic decision making in the organisation.
Manage the business intelligence tools including administration, installation, architecture, security and licensing for the Hospital and Health Service (HHS).

For more information about this role, please refer to the attached Role Description.

Vaccine Preventable Diseases (VPD) evidence as required for your employment in accordance with legislation/government policy and Directives.

What we offer:

Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health service offer a number of workplace benefits. Besides attractive remuneration, generous superannuation, salary packaging and career development opportunities, we value a healthy work/life balance that allows you take advantage of the sunshine, hiking tracks, swimming spots, reef and rainforest. 

Learn what it's like to work with us, in an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Discover our dynamic and culturally diverse region through community and lifestyle.

Listen to what some of our staff have to say about working in our regions.

Visit our website to learn more about our hospitals and health centres.
